zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Elizabeth Clynton於1523年出生在英國賀福爾萊奧明斯特時，她的父親Thomas Clynton10歲，母親Margery Tracy26歲。她於1544年與Reverend John Higgins - Knight在英國巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1596年1月12日在英國英格蘭賀福爾萊奧明斯特享年73歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Elizabeth Clynton于1523年出生在英国賀福爾萊奧明斯特时，她的父亲Thomas Clynton10岁，母亲Margery Tracy26岁。她于1544年与Reverend John Higgins - Knight在英国巴金哈姆爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1596年1月12日在英国英格兰賀福爾萊奧明斯特享年73岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clynton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Louth, Westmeath) and Scottish: shortened form of   McClinton  .

English: habitational name, either from Glympton in Oxfordshire, named as ‘settlement (Old English tūn) on the Glym river’, a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright stream’, or from Glinton in Northamptonshire, early recorded as Clinton (named with an unrecorded Old English element akin to Middle Low German glinde ‘enclosure, fence’ + Old English tūn). The surname was taken to Ireland in or before the 16th century.

History: Charles Clinton (born 1690 in Longford, Ireland) organized a group of colonists and founded the settlement of Little Britain, Ulster County, NY, in 1731. His son George Clinton (1739–1812) was governor of NY (1777–95), and they had many prominent descendants. — An English aristocratic family called Clinton was founded by Geoffrey de Clinton (flourished 1130), who held lands at Glympton, Oxfordshire. He was chamberlain and treasurer to Henry I (1100–35).

variants.name: McClinton
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Fhionntain (Scottish), Mac Giolla Fhionntáin (Irish) ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Fionntan’, whose name has been explained as ‘the white ancient’.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Elizabeth Clynton was born in 1523, in Leominster, Herefordshire, England, her father, Thomas Clynton, was 10 and her mother, Margery Tracy, was 26. She married Reverend John Higgins - Knight in 1544, in Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 12 January 1596, in Leominster, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Elizabeth Clynton est née en 1523, à Leominster, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Clynton, avait 10 ans et sa mère, Margery Tracy, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Reverend John Higgins - Knight en 1544, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 janvier 1596, à Leominster, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Elizabeth Clynton 1523 in Leominster, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Clynton 10 und ihre Mutter Margery Tracy 26. Sie hat 1544 in Buckinghamshire, England Reverend John Higgins - Knight geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. Januar 1596 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Leominster, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Elizabeth Clynton è nata il nel 1523 nel Leominster, Herefordshire, England, sua padre, Thomas Clynton, aveva 10 anni e sua madre, Margery Tracy, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Reverend John Higgins - Knight nel 1544, nel Buckinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 12 gennaio 1596, nel Leominster, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 73 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth Clyntonが1523年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアレオミンスターで生まれた時，父Thomas Clyntonは10歳，母Margery Tracyは26歳であった。1544年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアでReverend John Higgins - Knightと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1596年1月12日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアレオミンスターで73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth Clynton(이)가 1523년 영국 허퍼드실르 리민스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Clynton(은)는 10세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Tracy(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1544년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 Reverend John Higgins - Knight(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1596년 1월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 리민스터에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Elizabeth Clynton nasceu em 1523, em Leominster, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Clynton, tinha 10 anos e sua mãe, Margery Tracy, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Reverend John Higgins - Knight em 1544, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 12 de janeiro de 1596, em Leominster, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Elizabeth Clynton родилась в 1523 г. в местности леоминстир, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Clynton было 10 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Tracy, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Reverend John Higgins - Knight в 1544 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 12 января 1596 г. в местности леоминстир, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 73 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Elizabeth Clynton nació en 1523, en Leominster, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Clynton, tenía 10 años y su mamá, Margery Tracy, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Reverend John Higgins - Knight en 1544, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 12 de enero de 1596, en Leominster, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 73 años.
